# Code Graph Route Map

- Corpus: `cg-880c2146c31cd46c`
- Workspace: `/Users/tshinjeii/oss/kite`
- Status: `building`
- Built at: `2026-07-04T22:37:43+08:00`
- Stats: `989` nodes, `1779` edges, `217` files, languages `rust, typescript`

- Node kinds: `file=217`, `function=593`, `interface=119`, `repo=1`, `type=59`
- Edge kinds: `contains=988`, `imports=791`
## How To Use

- For narrow bug triage or a user-provided issue title, use `codegraph_search` as the structural locator for the primary symbol/file/concept. Then run `context_for` or `explore_area` on the best graph result before raw `read_file`. Inspect `context_for.insights` for peer comparisons, implementation candidates, and construction/injection paths. Treat returned snippets as already-read source and stop expanding once the root cause is clear. Use `grep_search` only for literal text, regex, log strings, or config keys.
- Start with an `Investigation Route` when the user asks a broad feature, architecture, bug, or impact question. Pick the route whose intent hints match the task, run its first query seed, then inspect the returned files/snippets.
- Use `Entrypoints` as runtime starting points for flow tracing. They are likely executables, handlers, listeners, schedulers, gateways, or lifecycle functions.
- Use `Domain Areas` as workspace regions. They narrow the search to packages and entrypoints before reading individual files.
- Use `Query Seeds` as ready-to-run tool calls for broad structure work. Prefer `grep_search` for exact strings, config keys, SQL, logs, regex, and path/include-scoped text.
- Treat `Confidence Gates` as verification reminders. Code Graph is structural context, so confirm dynamic dispatch, unresolved calls, and generated/external boundaries in source before precise claims or edits.

## Tool Routing

- Architecture/module/theme questions: `codegraph_query(code_graph_id="cg-880c2146c31cd46c", pattern="community_analysis")`, `cross_community_coupling`, `entry_flows`, or `risk_hotspots`.
- Named symbol/file/type lookup: `codegraph_search(code_graph_id="cg-880c2146c31cd46c", query="...")`, then `codegraph_query(pattern="context_for|explore_area", target="best-result")` before raw read_file.
- Callers/callees/impact/dependencies/path: `codegraph_query(code_graph_id="cg-880c2146c31cd46c", pattern="callers_of|callees_of|impact_of|dependencies_of|path_between", target="...")`.
- Compact source context: `codegraph_query(code_graph_id="cg-880c2146c31cd46c", pattern="context_for|explore_area", target="...")`; use this as the preferred follow-up to search and inspect `insights` before falling back to grep/read.
- Read files from `node.read_path` exactly as returned; do not prepend the workspace basename or copy `qualified_name` as a filesystem path.
- Literal text/config/error strings: use `grep_search` with normal text/regex arguments; it will stay on the text backend for non-structural searches.
